title: TimelineObject Object
description: Represents an object in the timeline.

methods.name: canReorder
methods.description: Checks to see if this object can be reordered to the specified position. The default value of -1 indicates the end of the timeline.   This method will fail if this is a timelineGroup object and the group is expanded.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: reorder
methods.description: Reorders this object to the position specified. The default value of -1 indicates the end of the timeline.

methods.name: rollTo
methods.description: Rolls the timeline by repositioning the marker to either before or after this object. This method will fail if this is a timelineGroup object and the group is expanded.

properties.name: entity
properties.description: Returns the entity associated with this timeline object. Edit operations can be performed by getting the object representing the associated entity and using the methods and properties on that entity to make changes.   Returns null if this TimelineObject represents a TimelineGroup object, since it does not have an associated entity.

properties.name: errorOrWarningMessage
properties.description: Returns the error or warning message in the case where the healthState property returns either WarningFeatureHealthState or ErrorFeatureHealthState. Otherwise this property returns an empty string.

properties.name: healthState
properties.description: Returns the current health state of the object associated with this TimelineObject.

properties.name: index
properties.description: Returns the position of this item within the timeline where the first item has an index of 0.   This property can return -1 in the two cases where this object is not currently represented in the timeline. The two cases are:   1. When this is a TimelineGroup object and the group is expanded.   2. When this object is part of a group and the group is collapsed.

properties.name: isGroup
properties.description: Indicates if this TimelineObject represents a group. If True you can operate on this object as a TimelineGroup object.

properties.name: isRolledBack
properties.description: Indicates if this item is currently not being computed because it has been rolled back.   If this is a timelineGroup object and the group is expanded the value of this property should be ignored.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets if this object is suppressed.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of this timeline object. This name is shared by the object the timeline object represents. For example, if the TimelineObject represents a Sketch and you change the name using the TimelineObject, the name of the sketch in the browser is also changed. The reverse is also true. Setting the name of an object; sketch, feature construction geometry, etc, will also change the name of the associated node in the timeline.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentGroup
properties.description: Returns the parent group, if this object is part of a group. Returns null if this object is not part of a group.

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Fillet Feature Edit API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates editing a fillet feature. To successfully run this sample you can use this Ruled Surface Feature API Sample

canReorder.title: TimelineObject.canReorder Method
canReorder.description: Checks to see if this object can be reordered to the specified position. The default value of -1 indicates the end of the timeline. This method will fail if this is a timelineGroup object and the group is expanded.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = timelineObject_var.canReorder()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = timelineObject_var.canReorder(beforeIndex)

canReorder.return_description: Returns true if the object can be reordered to the specified position

parameters.name: beforeIndex
parameters.description: The index number of the position in the timeline to check   This is an optional argument whose default value is -1.

classType.title: TimelineObject.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

reorder.title: TimelineObject.reorder Method
reorder.description: Reorders this object to the position specified. The default value of -1 indicates the end of the timeline.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = timelineObject_var.reorder()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = timelineObject_var.reorder(beforeIndex)

reorder.return_description: Returns true if the reorder operation was successful This method will fail and return false if this is a timelineGroup object and the  group is expanded.

parameters.name: beforeIndex
parameters.description: The index number of the position in the timeline to place this object before   This is an optional argument whose default value is -1.

rollTo.title: TimelineObject.rollTo Method
rollTo.description: Rolls the timeline by repositioning the marker to either before or after this object. This method will fail if this is a timelineGroup object and the group is expanded.
rollTo.return_description: Returns true if the move was successful

parameters.name: rollBefore
parameters.description: Set rollBefore to true to reposition the marker before this object or to false to reposition the marker after this object

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Fillet Feature Edit API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates editing a fillet feature. To successfully run this sample you can use this Ruled Surface Feature API Sample

Returns null if this TimelineObject represents a TimelineGroup object, since it does not have an associated entity.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = timelineObject_var.entity

errorOrWarningMessage.description: Returns the error or warning message in the case where the healthState property returns either WarningFeatureHealthState or ErrorFeatureHealthState. Otherwise this property returns an empty string.
